problem text: An archer targets a dartboard. Given a $\dfrac{3}{4}$ probability of hitting any single shot, determine the likelihood that she hits at least one out of her next three attempts.
problem solution: The complementary scenario is her missing all three attempts. The probability of missing each shot is $\dfrac{1}{4}$, so the probability of missing all three is $\dfrac{1}{4^3} = \dfrac{1}{64}$. Therefore, the chance of hitting at least one shot in the next three is $1 - \dfrac{1}{64} = \dfrac{63}{64}$.
problem hint: Consider the complementary scenario where she misses all three shots.
